text:
Once upon a time in a small town called Willow Creek, there lived a curious squirrel named Oliver. Oliver was known for his wide range of friends, who each had their own interesting tales.

One spring morning, ol' Oliver skipped along the leaf-littered paths, gathering seeds and berries. As he drifted through the garden, he paused beneath a great hornbeam tree.

There, among the blossoms, sat a book. It wasn’t just any book—it was worn, smelling of sunlit winters, and had lots of stains from where Oliver had nibbled on the corners. As he looked closer, he saw it belonged to Marilla Cuthbert.

Her name sounded so serious; surely she had written that herself?! Oliver thought.

With cautious feet, he popped the book open. It was full of letters. Pages and pages. They're all about chickens! Oliver squeaked with excitement.

That evening, he read until bedtime. It was a fun story filled with adventures. Finally,
